name: Generic Journal
notes: Standard journal format with natbib citations. No page limit.
writing_guide:
TONE: Use formal but accessible academic prose. Prefer active voice ('We propose...') over passive where natural. Avoid first person singular; use 'we' even for single authors.

ABSTRACT: 200-300 words. State the problem, approach, key results, and significance. One paragraph, no citations.

STRUCTURE: Follow standard IMRAD (Introduction, Methods, Results, Discussion). Introduction should funnel from broad context to specific gap to your contribution. End Introduction with a paragraph previewing the paper's structure. Discussion should compare with prior work and state limitations explicitly.

CITATIONS: 3-6 citations per section minimum. Introduction and Related Work are citation-heavy (10-20 each). Methods and Results can be lighter (3-8 each). Use \citep for parenthetical, \citet for narrative.

FIGURES/TABLES: Include 4-8 display items total. Every figure/table must be referenced in text before it appears. Use booktabs style for tables (no vertical lines). Captions should be self-contained.

REVIEWER EXPECTATIONS: Clear contribution statement, adequate baselines, honest limitations section.

DO NOT: Use vague hedging ('it seems that'). Do not start sentences with 'It is' constructions. Do not include unreferenced figures. Do not use footnotes excessively.
